summary: Decompiled five ftKirby SpecialN/copy-ability functions across four files. The PR body and decomp.dev bot both reported 100% matches; the bot credited +776 bytes of matched code and +0.02% GALE01 matched code.

key_files.notes: Adds EatWalk IASA logic for jump input, EatJump1/EatWait transitions, common animation/state setup, and walk fallback.
Adds Link/Clink copied neutral-special air end animation behavior and a boolean SpecialNSs motion-state guard.
Adds grab-timer update helper and includes ftCo_Bury.h for ftCo_800C0C88.
Adds Mewtwo copied neutral-special motion-state guard using ftKb_MS_MtSpecialN* and ftKb_MS_MtSpecialAirN* constants.

what_changed.details: Replaced a nonmatching marker with logic that reads ftCo_Jump_GetInput, stores non-none jump input into fp->mv.kb.specialhi.x4, transitions to ftKb_MS_EatJump1 with KeepGfx/SkipModel/SkipMatAnim, otherwise checks ft_8008A1FC for EatWait or falls back to ftWalkCommon_800DFEC8.
On animation completion, unsets Kirby Link bow/arrow state, chooses Link or Clink freefall toggle from ftKb_DatAttrs, then either enters normal fall or calls ftCo_80096900 with the toggle value.
Added a bool helper that returns true for null/default cases but, for motion ids 0x197 through 0x19C, returns whether fp->x2070.x2071_b6 is set.
Added a dont_inline helper that decreases grab_timer by percentTemp times ftKb_SpecialNYs_80109380, clears grab_timer for a specific dmg.x18CC/ftCo_800C0C88 condition, and sets fp->x1828 to 4.
Added a bool helper that returns true for null/default cases but gates Mewtwo copied neutral-special ground/air Start, Loop, LoopFull, Cancel, and End motion states on fp->x2070.x2071_b6.

smart_moves: Used small matching artifacts instead of over-cleaning: PAD_STACK(8) in ftKb_EatWalk_IASA, PAD_STACK(24) in ftKb_LkSpecialAirNEnd_Anim, and #pragma dont_inline around fn_8010B16C.
Preserved branch shape in ftKb_EatWalk_IASA with an explicit s32 var rather than simplifying directly to early returns, which likely helped match compiler output.
Used a goto end_true label in ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FCC6C to share the null-object true return path with the default true behavior.
Kept unknown or partially understood names such as fn_8010B16C and raw SpecialNSs motion ids 0x197-0x19C rather than inventing semantics not proven by the diff.
Added the narrow header dependency ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Bury.h only where ftCo_800C0C88 was needed.
decomp_lessons: Kirby SpecialN IASA callbacks can require exact post-transition helper ordering: Fighter_ChangeMotionState, ftKb_SpecialN_800F9070, ftAnim_8006EBA4, then ftCommon_8007E2F4(fp, 0x1FF).
Copied-neutral-special guard helpers may return true for null/default objects and only consult fp->x2070.x2071_b6 for a narrow active motion-state range.

For Kirby copied Link/Clink air-end behavior, freefall behavior is data-driven through ftKb_DatAttrs fields specialn_lk_freefall_toggle and specialn_cl_freefall_toggle after unsetting arrow and bow state.
Match quality sometimes depends on preserving access style: this PR uses both GET_FIGHTER(gobj) and gobj->user_data in different functions.
Do not remove apparently awkward control flow, stack padding, or inlining pragmas when they are serving the match.
naming_conventions: Kirby functions use the ftKb_ prefix.
Callback suffixes such as _IASA and _Anim identify action-state callback roles.
Copied-character shorthand appears in function names: Lk for Link, NSs for Samus-related SpecialN, NYs for Yoshi-related SpecialN, and NMt for Mewtwo-related SpecialN.
Address-suffixed helpers such as ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FCC6C and ftKb_SpecialNMt_80106F9C are retained when no better semantic name is established.
Unknown helper fn_8010B16C remains an fn_ address-name, consistent with not guessing behavior names.
Local names follow common fighter decomp style: Fighter_GObj* gobj, Fighter* fp, ftKb_DatAttrs* da.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Explicit temporary var in ftKb_EatWalk_IASA preserved branch/control-flow shape.
goto end_true in ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FCC6C preserved shared true-return layout.
Switch-case ordering was kept explicit for motion-state ranges and copied-character kind checks.
Float comparison against 0.0f and literal call arguments to ftCo_80096900 were kept as shown in the matched code.

review_feedback.feedback: Author reported ninja builds clean and fuzzy_match_percent: 100.0 for all five functions.
Reported 5 new matches totaling +776 bytes: ftKb_EatWalk_IASA +256, ftKb_LkSpecialAirNEnd_Anim +256, fn_8010B16C +104, ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FCC6C +80, and ftKb_SpecialNMt_80106F9C +80.
No human review comments or review records were present in the slice.

follow_up_queries: Find other ftKb copied-neutral-special helpers that return true for null/default gobj and gate active motion states on fp->x2070.x2071_b6.
Compare ftKb_EatWalk_IASA with nearby EatJump/EatWait callbacks to refine names for fp->mv.kb.specialhi.x0 and x4.
Look for callers of fn_8010B16C to determine whether grab_timer, x1828, dmg.x18CC, and dmg.x18D0 can be given stronger semantic names.
Check whether the raw ftKb_SpecialNSs motion ids 0x197-0x19C have enum names available elsewhere.
Search Link/Clink copied SpecialN air-end callbacks for the same unset-arrow/unset-bow/freefall-toggle sequence.
